title: Business Analyst
description: Business Analyst – FinOps Operations Dublin, Ireland \| Hybrid \| Contract via Coyle Consulting Our client, large Tech company in Dublin, is seeking a Business Analyst to join their FinOps team, supporting high‑impact operational programs that sit at the intersection of data analysis, case management, and business performance optimization. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a fast‑paced environment, enjoys solving complex operational problems, and can translate data into actionable insights. As a Business Analyst, you will contribute to analytical workstreams that drive measurable improvements in business performance. You will manage large data sets, support business case development, and produce clear, persuasive insights that guide decision‑making. Within the FinOps team, you will also take ownership of case management and service operations, acting as the primary link between vendor partners and internal sales stakeholders. This role combines analytical depth with operational execution, offering exposure to cross‑functional teams, vendor management, and continuous service improvement initiatives. **Key Responsibilities** • Conduct strategic and data‑driven analysis to identify opportunities that optimize business performance. • Architect metrics, synthesize information, and support problem‑solving across business functions. • Support or lead components of projects involving internal and external integrations between business objectives and operational systems. • Provide analytical support for ad‑hoc data requests from business leaders. • Evaluate new market, product, or cross‑team opportunities to support broader business initiatives. • Independently manage FinOps case queues, ensuring timely and accurate resolution of complex operational issues. • Host office‑hour consultations with internal stakeholders and close service gaps by designing workflows, resources, and processes. • Drive Root Cause Analysis (RCA) and continuous improvement efforts across vendor and operational workflows. **Minimum Qualifications** • Proficiency in statistical analysis, data analysis, and data synthesis. • Strong consultative and storytelling skills with the ability to translate insights into business recommendations. • Experience in project management and business operations. • Technical acumen, including familiarity with experimental design, dashboarding, and data presentation. • Domain‑specific knowledge relevant to business operations, finance, or technology. • Proven experience in case management environments supporting deal making, contracting, or billing systems. • Demonstrated vendor management experience, including workflow design and service creation. • SQL and data visualization skills (or the ability to learn quickly). • Experience with ERP/Billing systems such as Salesforce. • Ability to manage stakeholder expectations while balancing deep analytical work with front‑line operational support.

title: Business Analyst [12 Months]
description: **Position Description** The IT \& Digital area in Customer Solutions are responsible for all data, knowledge and technology platforms supporting the business strategy, working closely with our internal IT partners who are responsible for the delivery and implementation of all changes. Our technology landscape includes a broad SAP footprint, a wide range of digital technologies and other bespoke solutions and, at any time, a number of projects run concurrently across this landscape in addition to a program of operational change and process improvement. The Business Solutions team in the IT \& Digital area support the identification and scoping of changes required to deliver both short and long term goals, working closely with the business and our partners in Enterprise Services to deliver results in a timely and cost\-effective manner. The Business Solutions team wish to recruit a Business Analyst to support Electric Ireland and other customer facing brands. **Key Responsibilities** * Gathering, understanding, documenting and challenging business requirements and assessing their impact on existing processes, systems, data, and people in line with agreed principles and strategy. * Ensuring that the benefits of the relevant proposals are clearly articulated in a measurable way. * Providing input into the identification of both technical and process\-lead options to deliver business requirements and improvements. * Identifying and communicating with key stakeholders on a regular basis to understand their business needs and to provide updates on the progress of key work items. * Managing prioritisation of requirements to optimise business benefits taking into account IT landscape and other constraints. * Working with our Regulation team to understand the impact of industry wide decisions on Customer Solutions systems and processes. * Working with Enterprise Services and other delivery partners to identify solutions. **Essential** **Experience and Qualifications** * Minimum of 3\+ years’ experience in a similar role. * A relevant 3rd level qualification or equivalent work experience is essential. * Strong facilitation and meeting management skills. * Structured requirements gathering and documentation. * A high level of attention to detail, excellent organisation \& communication skills, both written and oral. * Initiative and ability to take ownership of own work through to completion. * Strong stakeholder management (Project, Business \& External). * The ability to understand core business processes and how they are enabled by related IT systems. **Desirable** * Business Process Modelling. * Structured problem\-solving methodologies such as Lean and A3 or a desire to become accredited. * Word, Visio, Excel, Sharepoint, Office 365 knowledge. * Experience working in a utilities environment. **Location** This position will be based in Swift Square, Santry, Dublin *Smart Working at ESB is designed to make ‘in\-person’ time together as purposeful as possible while also enabling the effective use of digital tools and hybrid arrangements to preserve our commitment to flexibility. The successful candidate may work flexibly within the jurisdiction where they have been employed, with attendance at the workplace for in\-person collaboration at least **two** days per week. All Smart Working arrangements are subject to regular review based on the operational requirements of the role, the team, and the business.* **Reporting To** IT \& Digital Team Lead **Why Work with Us?** * Opportunity to lead Ireland’s transition to net zero carbon future * Progressive, hybrid working model * Career development through mentoring and training * Corporate Social Responsibility Opportunities * Sports and Social Clubs * Networking opportunities * Credit Union * Generous Pension * Access to staff well\-being programmes * Generous parental leave entitlements * Strong values\-based and inclusive culture * Strong commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion * Great team environment working to our Core Values: Courageous, Caring, Driven and Trusted **Salary** €49,800 \- €58,600 per annum (depending on experience) **Closing Date** *1st May 2026* *Your application will be held in reserve for 6 months should you be suitable for the role.*

title: Business Analyst – FinOps & Operations | Global Tech
description: **Business Analyst \- FinOps \| Global Tech \| Case Operations** Not your typical Business Analyst role. This is for a hands\-on, operations\-focused BA who's used to owning and resolving complex cases in a large\-scale tech or enterprise environment \- not just gathering requirements. **What you'll be doing:** * Owning complex case resolution across billing, sales ops, and vendor workflows * Leading root cause analysis (RCA) and fixing recurring issues * Working directly with ERP / billing systems (e.g. Salesforce) * Using SQL/data to investigate issues and track performance * Partnering with sales, vendors, and internal teams to close service gaps * Building workflows and process improvements that scale **What we need (non\-negotiable):** * Experience in a global tech or enterprise environment * Strong background in case management/service operations * Proven track record of resolving high\-volume, complex cases * SQL (or strong data skills) * Experience with ERP / billing systems * Comfortable working independently in a fast\-paced environment **The details:** * €68K salary per year * 10\-month contract to begin with * Hybrid \- 3 days onsite (Central Dublin, DART access) * Onsite facilities, food, snacks plus amazing offices **Interested?** Apply today with CV for immediate review \- this role will go quickly

title: Business Processes Associate Consultant
description: **We help the world run better** At SAP, we keep it simple: you bring your best to us, and we'll bring out the best in you. We're builders touching over 20 industries and 80% of global commerce, and we need your unique talents to help shape what's next. The work is challenging – but it matters. You'll find a place where you can be yourself, prioritize your wellbeing, and truly belong. What's in it for you? Constant learning, skill growth, great benefits, and a team that wants you to grow and succeed. **What you bring** * 0 \- 3 years of experience in business process consulting and service delivery * Strong knowledge of industry and end‑to‑end SAP Intelligent Enterprise processes * Experience in data management, feasibility studies, and complex customer engagements * Project or program management expertise (PMI, Six Sigma, or equivalent preferred) * Strong communication skills and emotional intelligence * University degree or equivalent experience; SAP solution certifications preferred * Fluent English required; additional languages a plus **What you’ll build** * Translate customer vision into end‑to‑end business process and data management solutions * Evaluate industry and technology trends to shape innovative process and solution roadmaps * Design, map, and optimize business processes aligned to enterprise IT architectures * Lead solution design across SAP and non‑SAP landscapes (on‑premise, cloud, hybrid) * Drive feasibility studies, solution reviews, and innovation testing initiatives * Represent SAP with customers, delivering high‑quality outcomes on complex, high‑impact engagements * Support presales activities, customer workshops, and executive‑level discussions * Deliver and implement best‑practice business processes across LoBs and global rollouts * Drive change management and support transitions to S/4HANA and cloud solutions * Manage or support complex projects, ensuring milestones, quality, and budgets are met * Contribute to the evolution of SAP’s services portfolio, templates, and delivery assets * Act as (sub‑)project lead or team lead when required, using SAP Activate methodologies * Mentor and coach business process consultants, supporting people development and knowledge sharing * Continuously develop your expertise through certifications, innovation initiatives, and community leadership **Where you belong** At SAP, you will belong to a diverse and inclusive community that values your contributions and supports your growth. At Global Delivery Hub EMEA, our mission is to lead our customers to success on their business transformation journey to the Cloud. You will have the opportunity to work on critical customer projects, collaborate with experts across SAP, and make a meaningful impact on the success of our customers. Join us to bring your skills, build innovative solutions, and belong to a team that drives transformation and success. **Bring out your best** SAP innovations help more than four hundred thousand customers worldwide work together more efficiently and use business insight more effectively. Originally known for leadership in enterprise resource planning (ERP) software, SAP has evolved to become a market leader in end\-to\-end business application software and related services for database, analytics, intelligent technologies, and experience management. As a cloud company with two hundred million users and more than one hundred thousand employees worldwide, we are purpose\-driven and future\-focused, with a highly collaborative team ethic and commitment to personal development. Whether connecting global industries, people, or platforms, we help ensure every challenge gets the solution it deserves. At SAP, you can bring out your best. **We win with inclusion** SAP’s culture of inclusion, focus on health and well\-being, and flexible working models help ensure that everyone – regardless of background – feels included and can run at their best. At SAP, we believe we are made stronger by the unique capabilities and qualities that each person brings to our company, and we invest in our employees to inspire confidence and help everyone realize their full potential. We ultimately believe in unleashing all talent and creating a better world. SAP is committed to the values of Equal Employment Opportunity and provides accessibility accommodations to applicants with physical and/or mental disabilities. If you are interested in applying for employment with SAP and are in need of accommodation or special assistance to navigate our website or to complete your application, please send an e\-mail with your request to Recruiting Operations Team: Careers@sap.com. For SAP employees: Only permanent roles are eligible for the SAP Employee Referral Program, according to the eligibility rules set in the SAP Referral Policy. Specific conditions may apply for roles in Vocational Training. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to their age, race, religion, national origin, ethnicity, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, et al), sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or disability, in compliance with applicable federal, state, and local legal requirements. Successful candidates might be required to undergo a background verification with an external vendor. **AI Usage in the Recruitment Process** For information on the responsible use of AI in our recruitment process, please refer to our Guidelines for Ethical Usage of AI in the Recruiting Process. Please note that any violation of these guidelines may result in disqualification from the hiring process. Requisition ID: 452289 \| Work Area: Consulting and Professional Services \| Expected Travel: 0% \| Career Status: Graduate \| Employment Type: Regular Full Time \| Additional Locations: \#LI\-Hybrid

title: Business Test Analyst
description: **Test \& Business Analyst** **North Dublin – Onsite** **Salary 65k** We’re hiring for a leading organisation in the aviation retail \& supply chain industry to hire a Test \& Business Analyst to support the delivery of a large\-scale, integrated systems program. This is a highly visible role working across a complex environment spanning point of sale, retail systems, warehouse management, supply chain, and data platforms **.** **The Role** You’ll operate at the intersection of business analysis and testing, owning requirements, validating solutions, and ensuring systems are fit for release. Working closely with architecture, stakeholders, and third\-party vendors, you’ll play a key role in delivering end\-to\-end system quality across multiple integrated platforms. **Key Responsibilities** * Gather, define, and analyse business and functional requirements * Lead UAT planning and execution across change initiatives and releases * Develop and manage test scripts, test data, and test cases * Perform end\-to\-end system validation, ensuring data accuracy and integrity * Identify, log, and track defects through to resolution * Work with stakeholders to ensure solutions meet both functional and non\-functional requirements * Maintain and improve process documentation and system procedures * Support automation testing tools and continuous improvement initiatives * Facilitate workshops, meetings, and stakeholder communications across the business **What We’re Looking For** * 5\+ years’ experience in Business Analysis / Test Analysis / QA * Strong experience across UAT, functional testing, and end\-to\-end validation * Solid understanding of requirements gathering and BABOK principles * Experience working across complex, integrated systems environments * Strong stakeholder management and communication skills * A high attention to detail with a problem\-solving mindset **Ideally to have** * Experience in retail, supply chain, logistics, or aviation environments * Exposure to POS, warehouse management (WMS), or data platforms * Experience with test automation tools * Multi\-site or international project experience If this sounds like you, please apply today or DM me. \*\*No sponsorship on offer and must be able to go to the office 5 days a week\*\*

title: Business and Operations Apprenticeships in the Civil Service
description: **Job Title: Business and Operations Apprenticeships** **Employer:** Civil Service **Location:** Donegal, Dublin, Galway, Kildare, Laois, Leitrim **Closing Date:** 3pm, Thursday, 30 April 2026 **Salary:** Starting at €28,814 As a Business and Operations Apprentice, you will earn as you learn and gain a recognised qualification alongside practical hands\-on experience working for one of a variety of Civil Service organisations. Whether you are school leaver, starting out in your career, looking to upskill, or change career, this apprenticeship may be for you. The comprehensive teaching and learning experience will lay a strong foundation for long\-term career growth in the Civil Service. As a Business and Operations Apprentice, you will improve your skills and knowledge in areas such as: * improving operational delivery and business performance * budgeting and financial management * providing quality customer service to the public * project management and evaluating performance * the use of technology and data to improve processes * human resources and talent management. Throughout the programme, you will also build your understanding of how public services are delivered, engage with a variety of business functions, and improve your digital and leadership capabilities in a changing world of work. For the English language stream, positions are available in Dublin, Kildare, Laois and Leitrim. Some of the positions available in this competition will provide an opportunity for suitable candidates to develop their Irish skills further, through an Irish language stream in the following locations: Donegal (Gaoth Dobhair) and Galway (Na Forbacha). Candidates who successfully complete their apprenticeship will be given the opportunity to continue to develop their career at Executive Officer level, which is the entry grade to junior management in the Civil Service. A full list of essential requirements is available in the Information Booklet. The closing date for receipt of completed applications is 3pm on Thursday, 30 April, 2026\. We are committed to a policy of equal opportunity and encourage applications under all nine grounds of the Employment Equality Act.

title: Butcher
description: **Job Ref** 51924 **Job Title** Butcher **Job Type** Full time Location: Johnstownbridge, Enfield, Co Meath **Salary** On Application **Closing Date For Applications** 27\-04\-2026 **Job Description** **Main purpose of the role:** Ensure the Meat Department operates efficiently and effectively at all times and provide our customers with excellent quality and products and services. **The Ideal Candidate Will Have/be** * Craft butchery course is desirable * Numerical skills * Excellent communication skills * Previous customer service experience is an advantage * The ability to work as part of a team in a fast\-paced environment, ability to multi task under pressure * A passion for food and the ability to inspire shoppers * HACCP training is beneficial but not essential. **Main duties:** * Actively live Centra brand\-values i.e. Proud, Energetic, Imaginative and Community\-Based * Maintain retail cuts at a high standard in accordance with customer needs * Drive sales through ensuring SOPs and store standards are met on a daily basis * Merchandise and present department to the highest standard * Implement planograms correctly * Control stock rotation so that quality and freshness of goods is of the highest standard * Manage waste and shrink in the department * Ensure continuous improvement in the Meat Department by remaining up to date with new products and initiatives * Enhance product knowledge by gaining information from a range of sources and share ideas, suggestions and builds * Engage with new initiatives and embrace new ways of working. **About The Locality** O\`Neill\`s Centra is a family run store in the village of Johnstownbridge, outside Enfield, in Co Meath. We are now seeking an experienced Senior Butcher / Qualified Butcher to join our team and play a key role in the continued growth of the business. Key Responsibilities Retail butchery, meat cutting, boning, trimming, and portion control Preparation and presentation of fresh Irish meat to the highest standards Ensure full compliance with food safety, HACCP, and hygiene standards Support management across a two\-shop operation, maintaining consistency and quality Lead and support team members on the shop floor Contribute to product development, value\-added meat products, and seasonal ranges Deliver excellent customer service in a retail food environment The Ideal Candidate 3\-5\+ years\` experience as a Senior Butcher / Qualified Butcher / Meat Cutter Strong knowledge of traditional butchery and Irish meat provenance Experience in a retail butcher or food service environment Interest in innovation and modern food trends Reliable, professional, and quality\-focused Ability to work as part of a team and support junior staff What We Offer €40,000\- €50,000 (negotiable, depending on experience) Full\-time position 20 days annual leave No Sunday work No Bank Holiday Mondays Please note that the start and finish times stated are only an approximate Apply

title: Candidate Clinical Nurse Specialist (cCNS) in Occupational Health
description: **Role Title:** Candidate Clinical Nurse Specialist (cCNS) in Occupational Health **Job Reference Number:** 002289 **Closing Date:** Monday, 4th May 2026 **Purpose of the Role:** The purpose of this role is to deliver specialist nursing care in line with the five core concepts set out in the Framework for the Establishment of Clinical Nurse Specialist posts (4th edition), National Council for the Professional Development of Nursing and Midwifery (NCNM), 2008\. This role has been developed to support a change for specialist practice to develop and meet service need and in line with National policy as set out by “A Policy on the Development of Graduate to Advanced Nursing and Midwifery Practice” (DoH, 2019\). The successful candidate will work as a member of the multidisciplinary team providing a patient centered quality and seamless service. **Details of the service/Background to the post** The post holder will be required to work as a member of the multidisciplinary team in providing and developing an efficient and effective Occupational Health service for all Hospital personnel. Responsibility for excellent communication and the co\-ordination of service delivery rests with the Occupational Health nurse. The responsibility of the post holder entails not alone managing and enhancing existing services but identifying and implementing new initiatives to optimise staff wellbeing and an overall quality service. **Required:** * Be a registered nurse/midwife on the active Register of Nurses and Midwives held by An Bord Altranais and Cnáimhseachais na hÉireann (Nursing and Midwifery Board of Ireland) or be eligible to be so registered. **AND** * Be registered in the division(s) of the Nursing and Midwifery Board of Ireland (Bord Altranais agus Cnáimhseachais na hÉireann) Register for which the application is being made or be entitled to be so registered. **AND** * Have a minimum of 1 years’ post registration full time experience or an aggregate of 1 years’ full time experience in the division of the register in which the application is being made. **AND** * Have a minimum of 1 years’ experience or an aggregate of 1 years’ full time experience in specialist area of Occupational Health care within the last 3 years. **AND** * Have successfully completed a post registration programme of study, as certified by the education provider which verifies that the applicant has achieved a Quality and Qualifications Ireland (QQI), National Framework of Qualifications (NFQ) major academic Level 9 or higher award (equivalent to 60 ECTS or above) that is relevant to the specialist area of care and in line with the requirements for specialist practice as set out by the National Council for Nursing and Midwifery 4th ed. (2008\). **OR** Alternatively provide written evidence from the Higher Education Institute that they have achieved the number of ECTS credits equivalent to a Level 9 or higher standard (60 ECTS or above), relevant to the specialist area of care and in line with the requirements for specialist practice as set out by the National Council for Nursing and Midwifery 4th ed. (2008\) Occupational Health of care prior to application (\*\*See note below). **OR** If the applicant does not possess the relevant QQI NFQ Level 9 qualification, the applicant will be supported to undertake the required postgraduate education, at QQI NFQ level 9 qualification (equivalent to 60 ECTS or above) relevant to the specialist area. **AND** * Be required to demonstrate that they have continuing professional development (CPD) relevant to the specialist area or will be supported to obtain the required CPD. **AND** * Have the ability to practice safely and effectively fulfilling their professional responsibility within their scope of practice. **AND** * Candidates must possess the requisite knowledge and ability, including a high standard of suitability and clinical, professional, and administrative capacity for the proper discharge of the duties of the office. \*\*Note: For Nurses/Midwives who express an interest in CNS/CMS roles and who currently hold a level 8 educational qualification in the specialist area (equivalent to 60 ECTS or above), this qualification will be recognised up to September 2026\. The clinical experience requirements for this cohort of nurses/midwives remain consistent with the DoH (2019\) policy, i.e. a minimum of 1 years’ experience in practice and a minimum of 1 years’ experience in the specialist area and they could be supported to progress on a candidate CNS/CMS Pathway. All of the above must be achieved within 2 years for this pathway. The Candidate Clinical Nurse Specialist is required to progress to the Registered Clinical Nurse Specialist grade within 2 years of commencement of this pathway and formally apply for entry onto the Interim ONMSD CNS/CMS database (until the database is transferred to its permanent location). * Demonstrate I.T. skills including excellent keyboard skills. * Experience in teaching and assessing the Nursing and Health Care Assistant team. * Experience in carrying out audits. **Desired:** * Nurse Prescribing Registration \- or agree to undertake within an agreed timeframe, the nurse prescribing of Medicinal Products Certificate. * Have undertaken or agree to undertake, within an agreed timeframe, the Nurse Prescribing of Ionising Radiation Certificate. * Working knowledge of healthcare patient record systems.

title: Change Analyst Graduate
description: **Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions.** We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres and Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Our Graduate Programme:** We’re seeking the leaders of tomorrow to join us on our Graduate Programme \- those who are willing to share our values, live our culture and deliver beyond expectations. At Mercury we know that to achieve incredible things you must invest in your people. Our dedication to shaping the leaders of tomorrow shines through our highly regarded graduate programme and ensures we stay ahead in a competitive market. From day one you will be on a stellar career trajectory with no limits to what you can achieve, and the programme empowers you to develop the essential skills needed to excel in the fast\-paced construction industry. Since launching in 2009, we have successfully trained 360\+ graduates, many of whom have forged long\-lasting careers with us. Our comprehensive eighteen\-month programme is tailored to meet real\-world demands, featuring courses led by both external and internal subject matter experts in a focused learning environment. Graduates on our programme can anticipate working alongside a diverse and supportive team, accessing state\-of\-the\-art training and masterclasses, and gaining international experience at the leading edge of industry. An important aspect of the development programme is the completion of a group research project. Graduates are split into small groups of four or five and assigned a strategic business project to research and present their findings to Senior Management. All topics are chosen by Senior Management and are relevant to Mercury's business **Role Purpose** An entry\-level opportunity has arisen for a graduate to join our change management team. The Change Analyst role is ideal for an individual seeking experience in transformation, change management and business operations. The Change Analyst provides analytical, coordination and documentation support across Mercury’s change initiatives. This role helps assess impacts, prepare readiness information, produce governance materials, and support the rollout of Mercury’s Change Framework. **Rols \& Responsibilities:** * Business Readiness Support + Support the Change Lead in coordinating readiness activities across teams. + Assist in identifying impacts to people, processes and systems. + Help prepare readiness checklists, stakeholder maps, and cutover support materials. + Gather information from functions to track readiness status, risks and dependencies. * Change Framework \& Documentation + Use and maintain Change Framework templates + Support quality checks on documentation before submission to the CCC. + Help update templates and guidance materials based on lessons learned and feedback. + Maintain SharePoint folders, version control and documentation standards. * Communications \& Stakeholder Engagement + Draft communications, updates and briefing materials for impacted teams. + Support workshop preparation and facilitation (slides, notes, logistics). + Help track stakeholder questions, concerns and follow\-up actions. * Change Control \& Governance Support + Assist with data collection for impact scoring, risk profiles, and dependencies. + Track mitigation plans, decision conditions and follow\-up actions. + Maintain the change decision log and ensure it is kept up to date. * Portfolio \& Reporting Support + Assist PMO with data inputs for portfolio dashboards and reporting. + Track small\-scale changes and help identify collisions or overlaps. + Maintain trackers for readiness, risks, actions and adoption progress. * Continuous Improvement + Capture lessons learned and contribute to process improvements. + Suggest enhancements to templates, tools or ways of working. + Take part in developing Mercury’s maturing Change Function. + Conduct trend analysis on change initiatives approved and rejected **Essential Criteria:** * Graduate degree (Business, HR, Communications, Engineering, Data, or related field). * Strong written and verbal communication skills. * Structured thinker who can bring order and consistency to information * Comfortable coordinating with multiple stakeholders. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office and collaboration tools (e.g., SharePoint, Teams). * Ability to organise work and manage multiple tasks. * Interest in change management, transformation, PMO or business analysis. **Desirable Criteria:** * Experience in the Construction Industry or Trade Background ***Mercury is an equal opportunities employer.***

title: Chef de Partie
description: **Our Mission** We’re passionate about creating unforgettable dining experiences that delight our guests and celebrate the artistry of food. Every plate we serve tells a story—crafted with precision, creativity, and care. Our kitchen thrives on teamwork, respect, and a shared commitment to excellence, ensuring that every service is as memorable as the last. **The Impact You'll Make** As a Chef de Partie, you’ll be the guardian of your station, ensuring every dish that leaves it meets our exacting standards for taste, presentation, and consistency. You’ll mentor junior chefs, collaborate with the wider kitchen team, and bring your own flair to the menu—helping us deliver dishes that keep guests coming back. **What You'll Do** * Manage and run your assigned kitchen section (e.g., grill, pastry, sauce) with precision and efficiency. * Prepare, cook, and present dishes to the highest standard, following recipes and plating guidelines. * Train and supervise Commis Chefs, fostering their growth and confidence. * Maintain impeccable hygiene and safety standards, ensuring compliance with food safety regulations. * Work closely with the Head Chef and Sous Chef to develop new menu items and seasonal specials. * Monitor stock levels, minimize waste, and assist with ordering supplies. **What You'll Bring** * Proven experience as a Chef de Partie or strong experience as a Demi Chef ready to step up. * Expertise in your station’s techniques, with a passion for continuous learning. * Strong organizational skills and the ability to work calmly under pressure. * A collaborative spirit and a commitment to supporting your team. * Knowledge of food safety and hygiene best practices. **Why You'll Love It Here** * A supportive, creative kitchen environment where your ideas are valued. * Opportunities for professional development, including culinary training and cross\-station experience. * Staff meals provided during shifts. * Competitive salary and tips share. * Discounts on dining across our restaurant group. * A team culture built on respect, passion, and pride in our craft. Pay: €30,000\.00\-€35,000\.00 per year Work Location: In person

title: Cloud Security Architect.
description: At AIB, our values guide how we work and how we support each other. We’re looking for someone who puts **Customer First** , takes initiative and **Owns the Outcome** , and is always looking for ways to **Eliminate Complexity** . You’ll treat colleagues and customers with fairness and **Show Respect** , and you’ll thrive in a culture built on collaboration where we **Be One Team** to deliver meaningful impact. **Location/Office Policy:** Central Park, Leopardstown, Dublin 18 – Hybrid (3 days per week in office) * Want to play a key role in shaping the future of cloud security in a dynamic, fast\-paced organisation? * Are you a visionary in secure cloud architectures across public, private and hybrid environments? * Can you design cloud solutions that balance business agility with robust security controls? **What Is The Role** Reporting to the Head of Cloud Security, the job holder will provide support within the Cloud Security team, contributing to the engineering of security technologies to safeguard the Bank’s IT systems, network, and applications from current and emerging threats during development and maintenance cycles, with a primary focus on the organization’s cloud environments. The successful candidate will help implement security strategy and target security architectures, patterns, and roadmaps. The successful candidate will work closely with Security Product owners and project teams, supporting the alignment of our applications, products and services to our architectural and security targets as well. The role holder will be responsible for working across various cloud platforms, including public, private, and hybrid models, to assess risks and ensure that cloud controls safeguard the organization’s critical assets and sensitive data. They will be responsible for ensuring that the Security Products are aligned to targets and security architecture will be responsible for the roadmap of those targets. **Key Accountabilities** * Design cloud security processes, including risk and posture management, incident response, and operational resilience, ensuring successful and timely delivery of results * Design and implement robust security processes and frameworks to support cloud adoption, ensure compliance with ISO 27001, NIST, and CIS Benchmarks * Establish a framework for the development, review and implementation of technical security policies, ensuring alignment with regulatory and organisational requirements * Translate AIB’s cloud security strategy and business objectives into secure by design solutions. Maintain secure architectures for public, private, and hybrid cloud environments * Partner with cloud vendors, partner, architects, DevOps teams, and cybersecurity specialists to ensure secure solution delivery * Identify and solve complex security problems, recommending innovative solutions and applying in\-depth technical expertise to protect critical assets and sensitive data * Act as a highly specialised individual contributor providing expertise to stakeholders on cloud security related topics, mentor and guide junior engineers, fostering a culture of technical excellence and continuous learning * Provide technical expertise within the Cloud Working Group and Architecture Review Board, developing and presenting strategic technology solutions to safeguard the Bank’s IT systems, network, and applications * Champion cloud security, compliance, and resilience, embedding controls and monitoring in all solution * Drive cost optimisation (FinOps) and resource efficiency across cloud environments **What You Will Bring** * Minimum 8\+ years in IT security, with 3\+ years in cloud security architecture * Strong experience with cloud platforms (Azure, AWS) and their security services * Strong experience with cloud native technologies (AKS, EKS, OpenShift. Istio, Cilium) and their security services * Proficiency in DevSecOps practices and CI/CD security integration * Deep understanding of encryption standards, key management, and IAM * Knowledge of compliance frameworks (CIS, NIST, ISO27001\) **Preferred Certifications** * CISSP (Certified Information Security Systems Professional) * CCSP (Certified Cloud Security Professional) * CCSK (Certificate of Cloud Security Knowledge) * Azure Security Engineer Associate or AWS Security Specialty * Azure Solution Architecture or AWS Solution Architecture **Why Work For AIB** We are committed to offering our colleagues choice and flexibility in how we work and live and our hybrid working model enables our people to balance their time between working from home and their designated office, subject to their role, the needs of our customers and business requirements. *Some of our benefits include;* * Market leading Pension Scheme * Healthcare Scheme * Variable Pay * Employee Assistance Programme * Family leave options * Two volunteer days per year Please click **here** for further information about AIB’s PACT – Our Commitment to You. **Key Capabilities** * Leads Others * Instils Trust * Customer Focus * Information System Security * Technical Leadership If you are not sure about your suitability based on any aspects of the role advertised, we encourage you to please contact the recruiter Sarah Lyons at *careers@aib.ie* for a conversation. AIB is an equal opportunities employer, and we pride ourselves on being the first bank in Ireland to receive the Investors in Diversity Gold Standard accreditation from the Irish Centre for Diversity. We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations for applicants and employees. Should you have a reasonable accommodation request please email the Talent Acquisition team at careers@aib.ie **Application Deadline: Friday 1st May 2026**

title: Commercial Finance Analyst
description: **Description** Ryanair is currently recruiting for a **Commercial Finance Analyst** to join Europe’s Favourite Airline Group. This is a fantastic opportunity for a motivated and commercially astute individual who has excellent financial analysis skills to join our Commercial Performance team. The successful candidate will be integral to our growth plans as they continually review and optimise our network performance ensuring that our most profitable routes are prioritised allowing us to grow to over 4000 flights per day. The Commercial Performance Team is a small team with high value and impact to the overall business You will have exposure to all levels and senior Management to make a major contribution in helping us achieve our growth target of 300m passengers by 2034\. **Supporting The Senior Commercial Team, The Successful Candidate Will** * Continually analyse and identify new opportunities to improve financial performance, including detailed modelling of potential benefits and costs. * Responsible for performance improvement projects, including developing and maintaining analytical models and effectively communicating the results . * Propose and evaluate new business / route opportunities including forecast profitability and break\-even analysis for new routes. * Prepare competitor and market analysis reports for the senior commercial team. * Assist in preparing detailed monthly management reports including route profitability, which form the basis for all capacity allocation decisions. * Analyse and challenge proposed new airport deals. **Requirements** * 2\+ years experience in an accounting, commercial, financial or analytical role * Candidates who hold a ACA, ACCA, CPA or equivalent will be highly regarded * Excellent problem solving and analytical skills. * Commercial mindset with the ability to effectively communicate financial data and insights * Accuracy and strong attention to detail * Excellent Excel skills. * Proficiency in spoken and written English. **Benefits** * Competitive salary * Discounted and unlimited travel to over 250 destinations * Defined Contribution Pension Scheme – Matched up to 5% or €5,000 * Death in Service Benefit – Up to 2 times of annual basic salary * 20 Days Annual Leave – Increasing to 22 days after 12 months and 25 days after 3 years of continuous service. * Option for up to 5 additional unpaid leave days per year * Cycle 2 Work Scheme * Unrivalled career progression Competencies

title: Commercial Graduate Rotation Program - UK & Ireland Markets
description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* **About Futureforce University Recruiting** Our Futureforce University Recruiting program is dedicated to attracting, retaining and cultivating talent. Our interns and new graduates work on real projects that affect how our business runs, giving them the opportunity to make a tangible impact on the future of our company. With offices all over the world, our recruits have the chance to collaborate and connect with fellow employees on a global scale. We offer job shadowing, mentorship programs, talent development courses, and much more. **Job Category** Sales **Job Details** **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. **Start Date: August 2026** The EMEA Graduate Rotation Program is a new and exciting 12 month program which welcomes ambitious graduates who would like to begin their career journey within sales and solution engineering. It is a rotational program designed to give graduates exposure to both sales development and solution engineering. Having built the foundational skills and passed all required eligibility and readiness criteria, our graduates can then go on to pursue one of these two clear career paths. Our Top Graduates can potentially promote to the career path of their choice, (depending on business demand) by month 7 of the program. Our graduate program is a fully immersive program combining comprehensive learning paths, in\-role experience, customer interaction and mentorship from senior sales and solution engineering professionals. At Salesforce, you will have the opportunity to lay the foundations for a successful career in sales \& solution engineering and accelerate your career at a fast pace. We are a proud member of *Fortune 100* *Best Places to Work* list for the past 12 years and a consistent winner of *Great Places to Work.* **Who are we looking for?** * You are a 2025 or 2026 graduate (minimum bachelor’s degree). We consider applicants from All Academic Fields * You are interested in the impact technology can have on the world and business * You are a creative problem\-solver * You are a relationship builder and possess a growth mindset * Are curious, self\-motivated and focused on continuous learning * You thrive working in a team environment * You have excellent interpersonal skills * You are goal\-orientated and comfortable with a fast\-paced environment * You are fluent in English **What Does Our New EMEA Graduate Program Look Like** The Program is structured as follows: **Introduction Month** During your first month you will have extensive onboarding enablement in a Salesforce\-like academy, where you will: * Get immersed in the culture of salesforce and truly understand our values * Develop an understanding of our solutions, technologies and tools. * Learn critical professional skills for your career in salesforce * Structured learning calendar with sessions \& hands\-on workshops led by experts and program alumni **Rotations** During this Graduate Program you will have the opportunity to experience **the Sales Graduate Path** and **the Solution Engineering Graduate Path.** The structure for both are similar, for both you will spend 60% of your time immersed in the role, you will have clear tasks, clear targets and you will align with key stakeholders and the other 40% of your time will be spent on role specific learning. You will be paired with mentors/buddies with experience in the path. If during your first rotation (months 2 throughout to month 4\) are on the Solution Engineering Graduate Rotation, then you will join the Sales Graduate Rotation (from month 5 through to 7\) and vice versa. So in the these 6 months you will have exposure to both roles. Your time spent mastering the fundamentals of both these roles during these rotations will empower you to be even more productive and impactful when you eventually promote. Throughout the rotations there will be self paced certification and skills assessment. You will be required to meet all readiness eligibility criteria before you can Panel for Promotion. **An Example of what you can expect during the specific rotations** **The Sales Graduate Rotation** * You will be taught salesforce’s best in class prospecting and qualification methodologies * You will learn how to use social, digital, video, chat and phone platforms to effectively engage with Salesforce prospects * You will collaborative with Sales Development Specialists to run campaigns and help build sales pipeline for the EMEA Sales organisation **The Solution Engineer Graduate Rotation** * You will have the opportunity to learn our solutions at a deeper more technical level * You work closely with the solution engineering organisation supporting them in building cutting edge cutomer demonstrations * You will learn the art of customer discovery \& how to create a customer point of view **What’s in it for you?** * A clear and structured career path within our Sales and Solution Engineering organisation * Opportunity to work in an international team, within a nurturing environment * Opportunity to join our diverse internal employee groups * Comprehensive benefits package * Relocation budget and guidance in your relocation\-process * Additional seven days paid time off for charitable causes \- check out our 1\-1\-1 model **Map Your Future** With our Graduate Program you will have the choice of career paths * You can choose to take a Sales Path where after your Grad Program Rotations you can progress to Sales Development Representative, Business Development Representative and then into our Sales organisation as Account Executive, where you will position our solutions to potential customers. In some instances, this path may vary or be subject to change. * You can choose to take a Solution Engineering path where after your Grad Program Rotations you can progress to Associate Solution Engineer and rise to work at every customer level ESB, SMB, GRB. In some instances, this path may vary or be subject to change. * With our strong culture of mentoring we strive to develop industry\-leading sales and solution engineering leaders. This is a phenomenal opportunity to develop your international experience in an organisation that has transformed the Enterprise Software Market with Cloud Computing **Salesforce in Dublin** When we opened our first office in Ireland in 2000 we were one of the first digital companies to locate here. Today, our Dublin office has grown into a leading technology hub, and Salesforce is the global leader in customer relationship management (CRM), bringing companies of every size and industry together with their customers. Our new Salesforce Tower Dublin will bring our dynamic, multinational team together in the vibrant Silicon Docks area. **Accommodations** If you require assistance due to a disability applying for open positions please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form. **Posting Statement** At Salesforce we believe that the business of business is to improve the state of our world. Each of us has a responsibility to drive Equality in our communities and workplaces. We are committed to creating a workforce that reflects society through inclusive programs and initiatives such as equal pay, employee resource groups, inclusive benefits, and more. Learn more about Equality at Salesforce and explore our benefits. Salesforce.com and Salesforce.org are Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action Employers. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender perception or identity, national origin, age, marital status, protected veteran status, or disability status. Salesforce.com and Salesforce.org do not accept unsolicited headhunter and agency resumes. Salesforce.com and Salesforce.org will not pay any third\-party agency or company that does not have a signed agreement with Salesforce.com or Salesforce.org. Salesforce welcomes all. Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. title: Commissioning Engineer
description: **Commissioning Engineer – Dublin** (Nationwide Travel Required) Design Build Search is currently seeking a **Commissioning Engineer** based in the greater **Dublin** area to join our clients growing team. The company delivers projects nationwide, and the successful candidate will be comfortable with regular travel across Ireland as required. This is a fantastic opportunity for a recent graduate looking to become part of an established, close\-knit team with a strong reputation for delivering high\-quality projects. **Responsibilities** * Commission smoke and natural ventilation systems in accordance with project specifications and EN12101 standards. * Interface with BMS systems via Modbus/BACnet gateways. * Configure and test D\+H control panels (CPS\-M), actuators (e.g., CDC series), and accessories. * Perform site acceptance tests and final handover inspections with clients and consultants. * Produce commissioning reports, operation manuals, and compliance documentation. * Collaborate with project managers and installation teams to ensure timely delivery. * Assist in fault finding and on\-site technical support as required. **Requirements** * Electrical or Mechanical Engineering qualification (Level 6\+ or equivalent). * Minimum 2 years' of experience commissioning ventilation or building automation systems. * Strong understanding of electrical schematics and low\-voltage systems (24V DC). * Experience with control panels, smoke ventilation, or natural ventilation systems preferred. * Familiarity with EN12101\-2 / EN12101\-10 compliance is an advantage. * Valid Safe Pass and Manual Handling * Full clean driving licence if you’re interested in learning more, apply with your CV today for a confidential discussion.

title: Communications Intern - IE campaign
description: Are you curious, motivated, and keen to gain real‑world experience in a fast‑paced, supportive environment? We’re looking for an Intern who wants to learn, contribute, and make a genuine impact while building skills that will set them up for their future career. This internship is a hands‑on opportunity where you’ll work alongside experienced professionals, get exposure to meaningful projects, and see how your work makes a difference from day one. This exciting opportunity within communications will be for 8 weeks, starting on the **29th June 2026 \- 21st August 2026,** and will be based in the Dublin office. **How You'll Make An Impact** * To support the creation and project management of a UK campaign showcasing our projects and people, working to deliver the campaign deadlines. * To produce assets and compelling narrative, including digital (videos/images), which highlight who we are and what we do at Hitachi Energy in the UK. * To work across the organisation with UK ambassadors and project leads to ensure they are actively working to support the campaign in their Business Units. * Communicate effectively across multiple areas. **Background** * We welcome applications from students studying any subject who have a strong interest in communications and storytelling. You don’t need a specific degree — we’re looking for curiosity, enthusiasm, and a willingness to learn. * Ability to write clear, engaging content for emails, websites, newsletters, and social media. * Comfortable using Microsoft Office / Google Workspace; awareness of tools like Canva or Adobe is a plus. * Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines. * Brings fresh ideas and a willingness to experiment. **More About Us** The position will be open for applications until **30th April 2026** . The internship start date is **29th June 2026** . You will receive information about the timeline for the ongoing process in the notification after the first selection round. Hitachi Energy is dedicated to fostering an inclusive workplace where every team member can thrive and contribute their unique perspectives and skills. We provide competitive salaries, flexible working hours, professional development opportunities, and a supportive work environment that encourages growth and innovation through career development programs and Employee Resource Groups (ERGs). Specific benefits depend on the location and will be communicated during the interview process. Applications are open until \[insert date]. Ready to make an impact? Apply now and join us to inspire the next era of sustainable energy! Hitachi Energy is a global technology leader in electrification, powering a sustainable energy future through innovative power grid technologies with digital at the core. Over three billion people depend on our technologies to power their daily lives. With over a century in pioneering mission\-critical technologies like high\-voltage, transformers, automation, and power electronics, we are addressing the most urgent energy challenge of our time – balancing soaring electricity demand, while decarbonizing the power system. Headquartered in Switzerland, we employ over 50,000 people in 60 countries and generate revenues of around $16 billion USD. We welcome you to apply today.

title: Compliance Analyst
description: **Job Title:** Compliance Analyst **Department:** Compliance **Location:** Dublin, Ireland The Firm is seeking a Compliance Analyst to join our Compliance Department in the Dublin, Ireland office. The successful candidate will be responsible for conducting email reviews of trading groups and supporting various compliance projects. This role is critical to ensure the effectiveness, scalability, and robustness of the firm’s email monitoring program. **Key Responsibilities** * Conduct email communication reviews for trading groups and perform searches related to internal investigations. * Prepare weekly and monthly memos summarizing findings from email surveillance reviews. * Assist in the development and enhancement of policies and procedures to reflect changes in business, regulations, and emerging compliance issues. * Stay up to date with relevant regulatory developments and changes. * Participate in other compliance\-related projects as needed. **Qualifications And Skills** * Bachelor’s degree (preferably in Business, Finance, Economics, or a related field) with at least 3 years of experience. * Prior experience in a compliance role at a Registered Investment Adviser (RIA) or Broker\-Dealer is preferred. * Strong understanding of regulatory and self\-regulatory agencies (e.g., Central Bank of Ireland, FCA, SEC, CFTC, MAS) and familiarity with key regulations such as the Investment Advisers Act of 1940, Securities Act of 1933, and Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (FSMA). * Excellent team player with the ability to prioritize tasks in a fast\-paced, high\-pressure, and constantly evolving environment. * Entrepreneurial mindset with the ability to work independently and manage projects effectively. * Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to collaborate across departments and build effective working relationships. * Exceptional writing, analytical, and problem\-solving skills. * Proficiency with various technological applications and tools. * Broad knowledge of financial products and the financial services industry is a plus. * Experience with Microsoft Excel, Word, and PowerPoint.

title: Controls Engineer
description: **Overview:** We have an exciting opportunity for a Controls Engineer to join our Client's growing team. Your skills will be vital in providing leadership on projects and delivering best in class delivery for various project **Key Responsibilities:** * Critically analyse customer requirements to develop detailed software \& controls development requirements. * Work closely with hardware and software vendors to ensure the team are integrating the latest technologies into our designs. * Design, develop and test software to deliver on customer requirements. * Support and mentor junior team members. * Project ownership from management, communication, execution \& delivery of allocated projects, reporting back on project progress (technical issues / project updates) as required. * Lead meetings both internally and with our customers. * Support continuous improvement within the software department. * Support department goals and objectives. * Some travel required will be required. **Qualifications \& Experience:** * 5\+ years post qualification experience in a similar role or related role. * L8 Degree in Software development or related discipline. * Project Leadership Experience. * Experience of task estimation. * Experience of software development project planning. * Experience of software design for PLCs, Robotic, HMI and or Vision System applications. * Knowledge of test\-driven software development. * Experience of software development for PLCs, Robotic, HMI, Vision System and or Data Management applications. * Experience providing technical leadership to junior team members. * Full Clean Driver’s License is required. * Team player, ability to work in and embrace a highly motivated collaborative team culture. * Experience of developing software for automation solutions. * Experience of commissioning automated equipment. * Ability to debug automated equipment. * Excellent organisational \& project management skills. * Ability to provide technical leadership to junior team members.

title: Cost Accountant
description: **Company Overview:** The O’Reilly Group comprises three companies: O’Reilly Precast, O’Reilly Barleystone, and O’Reilly Oakstown. O'Reilly Precast is a leading manufacturer of precast concrete products in the UK and Ireland. With a focus on innovative and efficient modular building solutions, we have established ourselves as market leaders in the construction industry. Our commitment to quality, reliability, and customer satisfaction sets us apart in the industry. **Job Description:** We are seeking a detail\-oriented and analytical Cost Accountant to join our team in the O’Reilly Group. This role involves supporting financial visibility by reviewing project costs, recording stock, assessing the cost impact of remakes and enhancing cost accounting processes. The ideal candidate will contribute strong analytical skills, a proactive mindset, and the ability to collaborate across departments. **Responsibilities:** * **Precast Project Review Analysis:** Maintain and review project revenue and costs versus budget. * **Remake review\-** Work with the Quality department to maintain list of re\-makes and quantify costs. * **Inventory Management:** Review stock takes and monitor stock count procedures. * **Group Product Costing:** Maintain costs for group products manufactured, and analyse changes in product design, raw materials, and manufacturing methods. * **Support Functions:** Provide support for production system (MPM) and project management system (PMP). * **Cost accounting systems**\- Drive the redesign and automation of cost accounting processes to improve efficiency. * **ESG**\- Assist in tracking and allocating environmental and social costs (e.g., carbon emissions, waste management) to support ESG performance metrics. **Requirements:** * Competitive salary * Company pension * Bike\-to\-work scheme * On\-site parking * Private health insurance **Benefits:** * Competitive salary * Company pension * Bike\-to\-work scheme * On\-site parking * Private health insurance **How to Apply:** Please send your CV and cover letter to hr@oreilly.group. *O'Reilly Group is an equal opportunities employer. All qualified applicants are encouraged to apply.* Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company pension * Gym membership * On\-site parking Application question(s): * Do you have experience working with inventory, stock control, or manufacturing costing? * Do you hold a degree or qualification in Accounting, Finance, or a related field? Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Credit Analyst
description: 📝 **Company Mission** *Our mission is to give the world access to the best products by empowering great businesses to reach their growth potential.* 💼 **About Wayflyer** Today's SMB's need a capital provider that keeps pace with their growth ambitions. Traditional financing options are slow, cumbersome and often out of reach. That's why we built Wayflyer. Our unique technology allows us to assess businesses in minutes, generate financing offers that reflect their growth potential and send funds in as little as 24 hours. Since launching in April 2020, we've deployed over $5bn to thousands of brands worldwide, backed by Tier 1 banks like J.P. Morgan. We've become a trusted financing partner for some amazing brands, like True Classic, Little Words Project and Kekoa Foods. Teams at Wayflyer are truly cross\-functional. You'll be collaborating with ambitious colleagues from around the world \- all striving to deliver on a huge opportunity. Check out this video to hear directly from them. 💛 **Culture \& Values at Wayflyer** At Wayflyer, we value being sound people, excellent operators, and ambitious overachievers, working together with integrity, creativity, and bold optimism to deliver exceptional results. To learn more, please visit our website. 🚀 **Your Role at a Glance:** *The key ways you'll bring value to the team* * Analyse financial \& non\-financial data of new and existing customers to gain an understanding of their business * Understand and quantify the risks involved in running an eCommerce business and in Wayflyer providing funding to such a business * Support and help co\-ordinate regular calls with our customers to enhance our understanding of their business and funding requirements * Work closely with management to build and implement new processes and products * Support our sales and operations teams by understanding how they win and manage relationships with customers * Learn about our existing loan portfolio, our portfolio management tools, knowing when to exit risky relationships etc * Work with various departments and colleagues across the business **🔨 What Makes You a Great Fit** * 1\-2 years of relevant work experience (ideally in a finance\-related role, but not required) * You can demonstrate a consistent and strong academic record * You have an interest in credit risk analysis and portfolio management * You’ve completed an undergraduate degree in Business/Finance/Economics/Engineering/Law/Computer Science * You can analyse and understand financial reporting (e.g. management accounts / financial models) presented by companies * Comfortable leading regular internal and external (client) meetings and presenting findings and recommendations to Senior Stakeholders * You are commercial in nature, quantitatively inclined and can juggle risk mitigation with growth **How You’ll Stand Out** * ACA/ACCA/CFA/CIMA qualification * Studying towards (or intending to study towards) a professional accounting/finance qualification * Experience in MS Excel and/or SQL 🤝 **What to Expect After You Apply** You can expect to hear from a member of our Talent Acquisition team soon. If we believe you're a good fit, the first step will be a conversation with a recruiter who will guide you through the next stages of the process. The full interview process, which includes meeting the Hiring Manager and completing a case study or interview loop, typically takes approximately two to four weeks. 🏖️ **The Perks of Being at Wayflyer** 🌴 **Time Off That Matters** Recharge with 25 days of paid annual leave, plus public holidays. 🧘 **Your Wellbeing** Access support and resources to support your wellbeing, including our *Wellbeing @ Wayflyer* ERG. 🫀 **Comprehensive Health \& Protection** Stay covered with private healthcare, life insurance, and critical illness cover. 👵 **Secure Your Future with Our Pension Plan** Our pension plan helps you build a strong foundation for tomorrow, starting today. 🍼 **Family\-First Policies** We provide a generous parental and adoptive leave program for both Primary and Secondary caregivers, ensuring you have the dedicated, fully paid time you need to focus on your family. 💰 **Share in Our Success** With our equity scheme, you're not just an employee; you're a stakeholder in our journey. 🌴 **Work From Abroad!** Enjoy the freedom to work remotely from anywhere in the world for up to 60 calendar days a year. **📍Global Offices and Working Policy** 🇮🇪 **Dublin, Our Buzzing HQ** Our headquarters operates a hybrid model. The number of in\-office days depends on your team, but the majority of teams and our SLT are typically in 3\-4 days per week, giving you valuable exposure to senior leadership. 🇬🇧 **London, One Big Family** Home to around 30 employees, our brand\-new London office also runs on a hybrid model. You’ll often find our CEO working alongside the team with regular visits. 🇦🇺 **Sydney, Small but Mighty** Our close\-knit Sydney office has around 20 employees and follows a flexible hybrid model, fostering strong collaboration. **🇺🇸 Charlotte, our new US hub** Charlotte is our US headquarters and the foundation of our North American growth. title: Graduate Management Trainee - Dublin
description: **Overview** We’re Enterprise Mobility. A family\-owned, global mobility leader with a $39 billion turnover, nearly 90,000 team members, and operations in 95 countries. Led by CEO Chrissy Taylor, the third generation of the Taylor family, we’re built on a legacy that gives us the stability to focus on the long\-term success of our people, our customers and our business. **Why join the Graduate Management Training Programme?** As a Graduate Management Trainee, you'll have the freedom and support to explore your leadership potential \- and the opportunity to become a branch manager in one of our retail operations in as little as two years. In fact, most of our senior leaders began their careers in this very role \- including our current CEO. From day one, we’ll invest in you. You’ll be in a supportive environment where you’ll take on real responsibilities and gain invaluable hands\-on experience in customer service, sales, marketing, finance, operations, and more. **We work hard and reward hard work** Your work will be recognised with performance\-based incentives and opportunities for continued promotion. Our unique promote\-from\-within culture means you can keep your career moving forward without having to change organisations. **Award\-winning training and development** Whether you’re building on existing strengths or developing new ones, your growth is our priority. Through classroom learning, on\-the\-job training, and mentorship, you’ll have the tools and support to take the next step – and the one after that. **Our doors are open** As a Graduate Management Trainee, your experience at Enterprise Mobility will be as unique as you because you shape who we are as much as we shape your skills, path and potential. We pride ourselves on opening our doors to a variety of voices, giving way to an experience that’s both inclusive and that inspires diversity of thought. **Responsibilities** From your very first day, you’ll be trusted with real responsibility and exposed to all areas of our business. You’ll develop skills in: **Customer Service:** deliver exceptional experiences by confidently handling reservations, resolving enquiries, and building rapport with a diverse range of customers. **Sales and Marketing:** connect with local business partners, grow your network and develop lasting relationships. **Financial Control and Profitability:** understand the financial mechanics of a successful business, including cost control and interpreting profit and loss statements. **Operations and Logistics:** learn how to manage a fleet, plan strategically, and drive performance. **Leadership and Development:** take the lead in mentoring, training, and developing your own team \- with the opportunity to manage and promote others as you grow. **Qualifications** * A Bachelor’s degree is preferred; however, professional experience can be substituted if applicable. * You must have a full manual Republic of Ireland, UK or EU driving licence, but we do make accommodations for applicants who don’t drive due to a disability. * No drug or alcohol related offence on driving record within the last five years is permitted. **Additional Information** * In accordance with current employment permit regulations, we are unable to support an employment permit application for this role, as it is not eligible under the current system. * Regardless of your socio\-economic background, university attended, subject studied, and degree attained, we will always look at how you perform against our competencies and will judge you on that alone. * Please let us know about any accommodations you may need to participate in our recruitment process. * Please limit your application to only one job posting based on where you live and/or plan to work. Applying to multiple locations will delay your application being processed. * This job posting is for applications within the following location(s): Dublin

title: Graduate Surveyor (Cork)
description: **Who Are We** Founded in 1896, Knight Frank was registered as Knight Frank LLP a Limited Liability Partnership, on 3 November 2003, registered in England \& Wales at 55 Baker Street, London, W1U 8AN with the registered number of OC305934\. Headquartered in London, UK, Knight Frank is one of the world's leading independent real estate consultancies. We work responsibly in partnership to enhance people's lives and environments ***in over 50 Markets, 600\+ Offices, 20,000\+ People, 1 Global Network.*** At the heart of all we do are our Clients. A relationship built on trust is vital. As trusted partners in property, we act with integrity and care, understanding our clients’ unique needs and consistently thinking about the bigger picture to provide personalised, clear and considered advice across all areas of property. Through our deep understanding of key markets and sectors – both emerging and established – we are dedicated to meeting and exceeding their property goals. A true partnership is a balanced one, and our People are passionate about, and committed to, making our business inclusive and diverse. We give every individual the space and opportunity to perform at their best and be recognised for the immense value they bring. We empower everyone to have autonomy in their role and encourage them to use their voice in how we can make a positive impact as a firm and shape the future of real estate, the built environment and the impact on the communities we serve. Our collaborative and engaged teams provide excellent and dedicated client service. In our workplace, opinions are respected, everyone is invited to contribute to the success of our business and innovation, and new ideas are celebrated. In 2021 we celebrated our 125th anniversary and set out Our Desired Future, cemented in three pillars: People Potential, Client Centricity and always Creating our Future, together. Learn more about what sets us apart here. **About The Role** Knight Frank is seeking a motivated and ambitious Graduate Surveyor to join our Commercial team in Cork. This role offers an excellent opportunity to develop your career within a dynamic and supportive environment. You will gain exposure to a broad range of commercial property activities while working alongside experienced professionals. Full support, guidance, and resources will be provided to help you successfully pursue and achieve your APC qualification. **Responsibilities** * Assist with valuation instructions and inspections for all property sectors including investment, development land, office, industrial and retail for the purposes of secured lending and fund valuations. * Contribute to the delivery of all projects to include inspections, surveys, researching, drafting reports and valuations, inputting comparable information into our database. * Assist with agency instructions to include the sale and leasing of all types of commercial property. * Assist with the marketing of properties and pitches. * Assist with lease advisory instructions to include rent reviews and lease renewals. * Maintain and update databases, ensure timely responses and that all details are recorded accurately on the relevant system. * Utilise all relevant technology systems provided, maintain up to date and accurate records, ensuring valuations are continually and appropriately recorded. * Collaborate with the wider Commercial team, contributing to the delivery of the business plan, while supporting each other in the achievement of KPIs. * Identify and act on referrals as they present themselves across Knight Frank. * Live the Company Values in all dealings with colleagues and take responsibility for own development by undertaking activities to broaden experience and market knowledge, while also sharing your expertise and providing coaching and feedback to others. * Support the enhancement of the Knight Frank brand. * Develop client relationships and seek to build on our client base. **Requirements** * SCSI accredited degree. * Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, ability to multitask and meet deadlines. * Proficient in MS packages. Knowledge of relevant IT packages such as Argus would be an advantage. * Excellent report writing and numeracy skills. * PSRA Licence. * Full clean driver licence. Competitive salary Please note: this is a Direct Search led by Knight Frank. Applications from recruitment agencies will not be accepted nor will fees be paid for unsolicited CVs, even if provided by PSL agencies.
